MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-05-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MATS HAGSTROM, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND, FOURTH, AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. WHILE THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LACK OF INFORMED CONSENT IS A SPECIES OF MEDICAL MALPRACTICE, BY HER SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION PLAINTIFF HAS ESSENTIALLY ALLEGED A SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MEDICAL MALPRACTICE DISTINCT FROM HER FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MEDICAL MALPRACTICE. CACI 500 AND 501 APPLY TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, WHILE CACI 532 AND 533 APPLY TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGES THAT A BILATERAL CAPSULECTOMY IS A SUBSTANTIALLY DIFFERENT MEDICAL PROCEDURE THAN AN IMPLANT EXCHANGE. ABSENT ANY FACTS THAT ARE JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE THAT SHOW OTHERWISE, AND DR. HAGSTROM PRESENTS NONE, THE COURT MUST ACCEPT THAT ALLEGATION AS TRUE ON THIS DEMURRER. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE FOUR OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION (BESIDES THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION) MUST REMAIN THE SAME, AND NO NEW CAUSES OF ACTION MAY BE ADDED. DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Defendant Mats Hagstrom, M.D'S Motion For Summary Judgment Part 2 Of 2 (Added For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Mar-06-2017 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sasha Brower, of Shaghzo & Shaghzo LLP, Tel. 818.241.8887, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MATS HAGSTROM, M.D. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Since Dr. Hagstrom has only moved for summary judgment, and not made an alternative motion for summary adjudication, if Dr. Hagstrom has failed to satisfy his initial summary judgment burden on any of plaintiff Monica Perez's five causes of action alleged in her second amended complaint, the motion must be denied. Dr. Hagstrom failed to satisfy his initial summary judgment burden to show that Ms. Perez' first cause of action for medical malpractice lacks merit. To meet that burden, Dr. Hagstrom was required to present expert opinion testimony that he acted in accordance with the prevailing standard of care. Acknowledging this obligation, Dr. Hagstrom presented the declaration of an expert, Dr. Bernard Alpert. Ms. Perez objects to all of Dr. Alpert's opinions as lacking foundation. Those objections must be sustained since Dr. Alpert's opinions are based on Dr. Hagstrom's office chart and unspecified records from Fremont Surgery Center, Dr. Alocozy, Dr. Pousti, Dr. Green, Dr. Sontag and UC Davis Medical Center. (Alpert Dec. par. 5). The records from Dr. Alocozy, Dr. Pousti, Dr. Green, Dr. Sontag and UC Davis Medical Center have not been made part of the record on this motion and thus they may not be the basis for any of Dr. Alpert's opinions and Dr. Alpert's opinions are inadmissible to the extent they are based on those records. (Garibay v. Hemmat (2008) 161 Cal. App. 4th 735, 742). Moreover, although Dr. Hagstrom's office chart and his records from the Fremont Surgery Center have been made a part of the record on this motion, there is no evidence from Dr. Hagstrom or anyone else that those records are admissible under the business records exception to the hearsay rule and thus Dr. Alpert may not testify about the factual statements in those records. (Id. at 742-43). Because it is impossible to tell from Dr. Alpert's declaration what opinions he has, if any, that are germane to this case if his opinions based on his review of the records from Dr. Alocozy, Dr. Pousti, Dr. Green, Dr. Sontag and UC Davis Medical Center are excluded, all of Dr. Alpert's opinions are inadmissible. (See Weil and Brown book at 10:309 (moving party's declarations on a motion for summary judgment must be strictly construed)). Further, it is likewise impossible to determine which of the purported statements of facts related by Dr. Alpert in his declaration come from either those unauthenticated records and/or from Dr. Hagstrom's office chart and the Fremont Surgery Center records, so all of the factual statements in Dr. Alpert's declaration are also inadmissible because they relate factual statements from inadmissible documents. Without any permissible factual bases for Dr. Alpert's opinions, all of those opinions are inadmissible. In short, because Dr. Alpert bases his opinions on unauthenticated medical records that are not before the court and relates as fact statements in inadmissible medical records to support those opinions, Garibay mandates that Ms. Perez's objections to Dr. Alpert's opinions on the grounds of lack of foundation be sustained and this motion be denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. SASHA BROWER, ESQ. of Shaghzo & Shaghzo LP, Tel No. 818-241-8887 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. BRET R. LANDESS, ESQ. of Oium Reyen & Pryor, Tel No. 415-392-8300 appearing for Defendant Mats Hagstrom, M.D.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court vacates its tentative ruling as to the third cause of action, and adopts its tentative ruling as to the second, fourth, and fifth causes of action, ordering as follows: DEFENDANT MATS HAGSTROM, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to the second, fourth, and fifth causes of action. While the second cause of action for lack of informed consent is a species of medical malpractice, by her second cause of action plaintiff has essentially alleged a second cause of action for medical malpractice distinct from her first cause of action for medical malpractice. CACI 500 and 501 apply to the first cause of action, while CACI 532 and 533 apply to the second cause of action. The fifth cause of action alleges that a bilateral capsulectomy is a substantially different medical procedure than an implant exchange. Absent any facts that are judicially noticeable that show otherwise, and Dr. Hagstrom presents none, the Court must accept that allegation as true on this demurrer. Defendant's demurrer is SUSTAINED as to the third cause of action for intentional misrepresentation with 20 days leave to amend. In the amended complaint, the four other causes of action (besides the third cause of action) must remain the same, and no new causes of action may be added. Defense counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
